channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Designing for Mother Mary, a Fictional Pop Star Who Happens to Be Anne Hathaway
item.description: A costume designer and a couturier teamed up to create ethereal looks for Ms. Hathaway’s titular pop star in the new film “Mother Mary.”
item.dc:creator: Kellina Moore

item.title: Gwendoline Riley Would Prefer You Resist Assuming Her Life Is Like Her Books
item.description: The British author Gwendoline Riley may be as emotionally guarded as the women in her novels, which have caught on in America.
item.dc:creator: Thessaly La Force
item.media:credit: Billy Barraclough for The New York Times
item.media:description: The writer Gwendoline Riley in London ahead of the publication of her new novel, “The Palm House.”

item.title: Mamdani Says He Plans to Skip the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: In an interview with the local news outfit Hell Gate, the New York mayor framed his decision to avoid the glamorous fund-raiser as a way to keep his focus on affordability.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani might have seemed out of place at the Met Gala, an event that is widely considered to be the playground of the rich and famous.

item.title: At Coachella, the Bob Baker Marionette Theater Makes Its Debut
item.description: After years of struggling to keep the doors open, the Bob Baker Marionette Theater joined a lineup that included Justin Bieber and Katseye.
item.dc:creator: Juno Carmel
item.category: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Alien puppets rehearsing the song “Soda Pop” from “KPop Demon Hunters.”

item.title: How Do You Decide Which Clothes Are Worth the Price?
item.description: Tell us about how you decide to spend your money and what is worth the splurge.
item.dc:creator: Kellina Moore
item.media:credit: Getty Images

item.title: What We Know About Clavicular’s Apparent Overdose and Hospitalization
item.description: A harrowing incident involving Clavicular, ambassador to the “looksmaxxing” community, was captured on the same platforms that made him a star.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bernstein
item.media:credit: Cassidy Araiza for The New York Times
item.media:description: Braden Peters, better known as Clavicular, was carried out of a bar in Miami this week after an apparent overdose. Not long after, he was back out on the town for a club event.

item.title: Aging in a Brightly Lit, Big City: Jay McInerney, With a New Novel
item.description: In 1984, Jay McInerney was a famous, young, hedonistic novelist. Now 71, he is wistful as he wraps up his tetralogy about a couple whose city, and marriage, are tested by the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: David Daley
item.category: See You on the Other Side (Book)
item.media:credit: Gus Powell for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jay McInerney, author of “See You on the Other Side,” at The Odeon restaurant in Tribeca.

item.title: New York Bridal Fashion Week Spring 2026: Trends Steeped in Romance and Nostalgia
item.description: New silhouettes and brands, a focus on gothic romance and the resurgence of the ‘90s style slip dress were just some of the many highlights from the runways.
item.dc:creator: Ivy Manners

item.title: He Reinvented the Hoodie. (Good Luck Getting One.)
item.description: Jake Burt sells his wares at a single shop that he staffs himself, stoking interest through scarcity.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Charlotte Hadden for The New York Times

item.title: Activists Urge Boycott of ‘Bezos Met Gala’ With Posters Around NYC
item.description: A guerrilla activist group is papering the city with posters criticizing the billionaire Jeff Bezos’ involvement in the event, a fund-raiser for the Metropolitan Museum.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta

item.title: Before They Met, Their Parents Had Already Known Each Other
item.description: When Emily Wiser first met Griffin Lear, she was excited to tell her father that she was dating a Dartmouth grad like him. Then they shared some history of their own about Mr. Lear’s parents.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: Painting Over My Painful Past
item.description: My father treated me more like a wife than a child. It took me decades to face the truth of what happened.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Hanssen
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: A Duet Decades in the Making
item.description: After meeting for a website project in 2020, James Squires and Jacqui Agostinelli bonded over their love of music, forming both a marriage and their musical duo, Sweet Basil.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Her Grandmother Told Her ‘Maybe You’ll Marry Him One Day’
item.description: After Ben Sweeney and Lauren Fogel debarked their plane at 3 a.m., he invited her to stay at his parents’ house nearby. Her grandmother was impressed.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: It Took a Persistent Friend and a Dave Matthews Concert to Seal the Deal
item.description: Daniel Martinez Clavijo and Johanna Mele finally met in 2021, thanks to their mutual friend, who had been trying to set them up for almost a decade, and a chance encounter at Madison Square Garden.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: On ‘The Pitt,’ the Lingering Effects of Trauma Take the Spotlight
item.description: On top of the daily toll of treating patients, the show’s medical providers bring their own scars to the E.R.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron

item.title: What Heelys, the Sneakers With Wheels, Taught Me About Momentum as a Child
item.description: Heelys, the sneakers with wheels that were a fad in the 2000s, helped me form my first friendships. Recently, I decided to tap into that joy again.
item.dc:creator: Ash Wu
item.media:credit: Amy Padnani/The New York Times
item.media:description: Ash Wu wearing a pair of Heelys. Reconnecting with the shoes brought back memories of her childhood.

item.title: Roger Adams Dies at 71; Invented the Rolling Sneakers Known as Heelys
item.description: You could walk in them like gym shoes, but if you rocked back on your heels the wheels emerged, turning them into roller skates. In the early 2000s, the company sold millions.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: What We Lose When Everything Is ‘-Coded’
item.description: On the social internet, our fascination with analyzing the hidden messages in our culture has been flattened into one word.
item.dc:creator: Dan Brooks

item.title: What Was the Deal With Jack Harlow’s Giant Hat?
item.description: Also, fashion can’t quit Anna Wintour.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: The Good List: 7 Things to Add Some Delight to Your Day
item.description: Train jazz, the “Brady Bunch” house and the gift of time.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘Embarrassed Yet Unable to Stop’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: My Sister Excluded Me From Our Family Holiday. Help!
item.description: After years of having to miss faraway holiday get-togethers, a reader was finally denied an invitation to his sister’s Easter gathering altogether.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes
item.media:credit: Miguel Porlan

item.title: Elizabeth Banks Reflects on 33 Years of Marriage
item.description: The “Miniature Wife” star on why she and her husband have chosen each other, over and over again.
item.dc:creator: Anna Martin, Elisa Gutierrez, Emily Lang, Lynn Levy, Daniel Ramirez and Dan Powell
item.media:credit: Maggie Shannon for The New York Times
item.media:description: The actress Elizabeth Banks.

item.title: Why Is Everyone Wicked Obsessed With This Boston Globe Reporter?
item.description: Emily Sweeney, a veteran journalist at the newspaper, is a social media sensation, bringing joy to all with her “hahd” Boston accent.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Pilat

item.title: Alex Cooper and Alix Earle Are Fighting. Or Are They?
item.description: Two popular influencers have exchanged jabs on social media.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta
item.media:credit: From left, Neilson Barnard/Getty Images; Daniel Cole/Reuters
item.media:description: Alix Earle, left, and Alex Cooper.

item.title: The Podcaster Recho Omondi Has No Hang-Ups About Dining Alone
item.description: The designer and host of “The Cutting Room Floor” fashion podcast answers the Where to Eat questionnaire.
item.dc:creator: Nikita Richardson
item.media:credit: Jamie McCarthy/Getty Images

item.title: Designer Fashion Hits the 2026 WNBA Draft
item.description: Fashion brands have taken note of the WNBA draft, described by Lauren Betts, the No. 4 draft pick, as the Met Gala of women’s basketball. Vanessa Friedman, our chief fashion critic, was there.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman, Gabriel Blanco, Nikolay Nikolov, Laura Salaberry and Bernardo Garcia Elguezabal

item.title: Adriano Goldschmied, ‘Godfather’ of Modern Denim, Dies at 82
item.description: An energetic entrepreneur, he developed more than 50 brands of jeans, including Gap 1969 and other cult classics.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green

item.title: Have Mental Health Concerns Influenced Your Choice to Have Children?
item.description: If you are on the fence, or have already made a decision, we want to hear about it.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron
item.media:credit: Getty Images

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Caramel Leather Jacket
item.description: A worn, leathery jacket was the centerpiece of a thoughtfully accessorized ensemble.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: How Do You Make a Medieval Film on a Shoestring Budget?
item.description: Caroline Golum used a crowdfunding site and a credit card to raise funds for her movie about a 14th-century mystic. Then she started filming in Queens.
item.dc:creator: Paul McAdory
item.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
item.media:description: The filmmaker at home in Brooklyn.

item.title: I Feel So Sorry for My A.I. Sunglasses
item.description: Plenty of people hate Mark Zuckerberg’s superintelligent, supercharged spectacles. I was ready to hate them, too.
item.dc:creator: Sam Anderson

item.title: Geneva Watch Week Seeks to Brighten Watchmaking’s Future
item.description: At Watches and Wonders, dozens of brands will be introducing new collections.
item.dc:creator: Robin Swithinbank

item.title: The TAG Heuer Monaco Evergraph Sparks a Revolution
item.description: The timepiece updates the square-cased watch design from the 1960s with an inventive chronograph movement.
item.dc:creator: Robin Swithinbank

item.title: The New Aesthetic of ‘Euphoria’
item.description: “Euphoria,” the HBO Max show depicting Gen Z, has released its final season. Three of our Style reporters — Gina Cherelus, Jacob Gallagher and Callie Holtermann — discuss the show’s new western aesthetic.
item.dc:creator: Gina Cherelus, Jacob Gallagher, Callie Holtermann, Léo Hamelin and Gabriel Blanco

item.title: 11 Best Coachella 2026 Looks: Justin Bieber, Addison Rae and More
item.description: The fashion at the festival is rarely rooted in reality. That’s what makes it fun.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Rotunno
item.category: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
item.media:credit: Arturo Holmes/Getty Images
item.media:description: FKA Twigs’s performance attire included a chain-mail hood that she removed to reveal tightly braided red hair.

item.title: ‘You Me & Tuscany’ Stars, Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page, Embrace ‘Cheesy’ Rom-Coms
item.description: The stars of “You, Me & Tuscany” make the case for messy, vulnerable and sometimes awkward romance. “I want to give people the freedom to cringe again,” Mr. Page said.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan
item.media:credit: Giulia Parmigiani/Universal Pictures, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Anna (Halle Bailey) and Michael (Regé-Jean Page) in a scene from the romantic comedy “You, Me & Tuscany,” which opened in theaters on April 10.

item.title: That Winged Eyeliner Makes You Look So … Millennial
item.description: In contrast to Gen Z’s “clean girl aesthetic,” the cat eye of yesteryear has become a generational signifier.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy
item.media:credit: Scott a Garfitt/Invision, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Popularized by millennial celebrities like Taylor Swift, the winged eyeliner look rose to prominence in the 2010s before giving way to the more natural look favored by Gen Z.

item.title: ‘We Are Growing’: Fresh Designs Lift Raymond Weil
item.description: Elie Bernheim, the chief executive of Raymond Weil, has confidence in his “accessible luxury” business model.
item.dc:creator: Robin Swithinbank
item.media:credit: Darren S. Higgins for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I have one single objective,” said Elie Bernheim, the chief executive of Raymond Weil, his family’s watch brand. “To pass the baton one day to a fourth generation.

item.title: A Cartier Design Worn by Grace Kelly Evolves Into a Timepiece
item.description: The Grain de Café, or coffee bean, motif from the 1930s has resurfaced in a watch.
item.dc:creator: Milena Lazazzera
item.media:credit: Silver Screen Collection/Getty Images
item.media:description: The Grain de Café motif became widely recognized when Grace Kelly was seen wearing it while promoting her final film, “High Society.”

item.title: At Van Cleef & Arpels, Movement and Craftsmanship Tell the Story
item.description: Rainer Bernard of Van Cleef & Arpels explains that when it comes to refined horology, storytelling is at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: Ming Liu
item.media:credit: Clara Watt for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rainer Bernard, head of research and development for watchmaking at Van Cleef & Arpels, at the company’s ateliers in Geneva in March.

item.title: In Minneapolis, Following Prince’s ‘Purple Trail’
item.description: This year is the 10th anniversary of the artist’s death. We made a pilgrimage to the city where he lived and worked.
item.dc:creator: David Farley
item.media:credit: Caroline Yang for The New York Times
item.media:description: For fans of Prince, Minneapolis, the artist’s hometown, is an essential destination. Above, a mural of Prince, painted in 2022 by the street artist Hiero Veiga, in downtown Minneapolis.

item.title: Shaping the New Look of ‘Euphoria’
item.description: Natasha Newman-Thomas has stepped in as the new costume designer for the third season of HBO’s chaotic megahit. It’s a lofty task.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: A New Watch Fair Arrives in Geneva
item.description: A cohort of younger brand leaders has teamed up to present ‘a new wave of horology’ at Chronopolis.
item.dc:creator: Allen Farmelo

item.title: March LA.B is a Watch Brand With a Decidedly French Swagger
item.description: March LA.B, founded in 2009 to fill a gap in the market between “the small guys and big guys,” is making its Watches and Wonders Geneva debut.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Felder
item.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times
item.media:description: Necklace and wrist versions of the Lady Volante, a chunky quartz-powered watch style by French brand March LA.B.

item.title: How Creators Attend Coachella, the ‘Influencer Olympics’: Begging, Manifesting and Dumb Luck
item.description: Begging, manifesting, sheer dumb luck: Influencers will try anything to get someone to pay their way to the California music festival.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.category: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
item.media:credit: Ye Fan for The New York Times
item.media:description: All-expenses-paid brand trips to the Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival have become more scarce over the years. “Brands have picked up on how thirsty creators are to go to Coachella,” one influencer manager said. “They’re realizing they don’t have to pay them.”

item.title: Petra Collins Is Ready to Give Her ‘STAR’ Power
item.description: In her new book “STAR,” Petra Collins, a photographer who helped shape the millennial aesthetic, lets us peek through her diffuse lens.
item.dc:creator: Lizzy Goodman
item.media:credit: Magdalena Wosinska for The New York Times

item.title: Alpine Divorce: A Hike That Ends a Relationship
item.description: The “Alpine divorce,” in which one partner leaves another stranded while hiking, is more serious than the name implies.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova
item.media:credit: Getty Images
item.media:description: Hiking guides and mountain rescue workers say what’s now being called “Alpine divorce” isn’t new. They’ve been seeing it for years. Long before it became a social media buzzword, they would often have to rescue women left behind on remote and often dangerous trails.

item.title: Maybe You’ve Seen This Hat
item.description: The green and cream cap appears on the heads of guys everywhere who want to dabble in a trend.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Raimonda Kulikauskiene/Getty Images
item.media:description: It’s sporty and nostalgic, with a touch of TikTok. Its ubiquity on social media has helped it travel far.

item.title: At Houston’s Art Car Parade, Trashed Vehicles Become Everyone’s Treasure
item.description: Texans spend a lot of time in their cars. But once a year, they converge at the Art Car Parade to celebrate those who transform rundown vehicles into masterpieces on wheels.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Sims and Hope Mora
item.media:credit: Hope Mora for The New York Times

item.title: Luxury Watch Factories Do More Than Produce; They Sell Stories
item.description: Watchmakers are investing in new manufacturing sites to signal expertise and court clients.
item.dc:creator: Milena Lazazzera
item.category: LVMH Moet Hennessy Louis Vuitton SA
